description_long: This is a standard Western-style toilet with a rounded bowl and an attached cistern at the back. The toilet features a raised seat and lid, both of which are currently in the upright position, revealing the interior of the bowl. The cistern is rectangular and sits directly behind the bowl, with a flush lever visible on the left side. The base is broad and flat, designed for stable floor mounting, and the overall surface appears to be made of glazed ceramic with some visible discoloration and wear. The seat and lid are likely made of plastic, contrasting slightly in texture and color with the ceramic body. The toilet is typical of those found in public restrooms, residential bathrooms, or urban facilities.
description: A floor-mounted Western-style toilet with a rounded bowl, upright seat and lid, and a rectangular exposed cistern. The surfaces show signs of wear and discoloration, with a visible flush lever on the cistern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open seat and lid, the bowl interior, and the cistern behind.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the bowl, the upright seat and lid, and the flush lever on the cistern.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cistern and the base, with no visible interactive parts.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side profile of the bowl and cistern, with the seat and lid upright.
